title: Monetary Policy Report—October 2024
summary: The Bank of Canada reduced its target for the overnight rate by 50 basis points to 3¾% (Bank Rate at 4%, deposit rate at 3¾%), citing that CPI inflation has fallen to around 2% and is expected to remain near target over the projection horizon. The Canadian economy remains in excess supply with a softening labour market, while growth is projected to average 2¼% over 2025-2026 as interest rate decreases support consumer spending and business investment.
key_points: Bank of Canada cut the overnight policy rate by 50 bps to 3¾% (Bank Rate 4%, deposit rate 3¾%)
CPI inflation has fallen to around 2%, within the Bank's control range of 1-3%, with core inflation forecast to decline gradually
Canadian economy continues to be in excess supply; GDP per person continues to decline
Labour market has softened with unemployment increases concentrated among newcomers and youth; wage growth remains elevated relative to productivity
Canada's economic growth expected to pick up to 2¼% average over 2025 and 2026, supported by rate decreases
Global economy expected to grow at around 3% over the projection horizon, with US growth strong but slowing, euro area subdued, and China facing property crisis weakness
why_it_matters: This quarterly monetary policy report from the Bank of Canada provides the central bank's comprehensive assessment of economic conditions and forward guidance. The 50 bps rate cut represents a significant easing move, signaling the Bank's confidence that inflation is sustainably near target. Market participants monitor these reports for changes to growth forecasts, inflation projections, and risk assessments that could signal future policy direction.
macro_context: The Bank is equally concerned with inflation rising above or falling below the 2% target, indicating a balanced risk posture. Energy exports are rising while business investment and government spending growth are slowing. The global environment includes weak growth in China weighing on oil demand, with global oil prices decreased and volatile. US GDP growth is strong but expected to slow, while euro area growth remains subdued but is projected to rise gradually.

issues_found: Source is a press release summarizing the MPR rather than the full report document—detailed projection tables and complete risk analysis from the full MPR are not included

human_review.notes: Source is a Bank of Canada press release summarizing the October 2024 Monetary Policy Report. While it contains the key rate decision and high-level economic assessment, this is the press release format rather than the full MPR document. The July 2024 forecast comparison, detailed projections tables, and risk analysis from the full report are not included in this source body. Consider retrieving the full PDF for complete projection details.

notes: Historical record from October 2024. This is the Bank of Canada's Monetary Policy Report press release, summarizing the October 23, 2024 policy decision and economic assessment. The source includes the Governing Council (Tiff Macklem, Carolyn Rogers, Toni Gravelle, Sharon Kozicki, Nicolas Vincent, Rhys Mendes) decision to cut rates by 50 bps. The source is relevant to macro catalysts as it provides the Bank of Canada's official assessment of Canadian and global economic conditions and their inflation outlook. Not a market structure document — no discussion of market plumbing, operations, or structural market issues.
